title: "brushless low dc current high torque motor and regenerator with controller
abstract: Disclosed is a brushless low DC current motor and regenerator with controller for high torque generation. The brushless direct current motor (124) includes a first control unit (104) to receive inputs from a first input unit (108), a second input unit (112), a third input unit (116), and power from a battery (120) to control the speed of the motor (124), to generate the required torque. The motor (124) includes an inner stator (304), an outer stator (308) and a rotor (312) coaxially rotatable around the motor axis. The inner stator (304) and the outer stator (308) is coaxially attached with the motor and have equal number of teethes. The plurality of magnets (324) is positioned on the rotor (312).
claims:
Claims 1. A brushless low DC current high torque motor and regenerator (100) with controller for controlling power from battery comprising: a first control unit (104), the first control unit (104) being configured on a motor (124) for transmitting, and receiving the signals, for controlling power from battery (120) to the motor (124), the first control unit (104) including: a first controller (200), the first controller 200 being configured on the first control unit (104) for receiving, a current input from a first input (108), a user predefined speed signal, a user predefined speed direction and a user predefined current limit value from a second input (112), a rotor position signal with respect to a stators signal from the motor (124), and a power from the battery (120), in a first mode of torque generation, for monitoring current consumption by the motor (124), for generating controlled high torque and transferring analyzed controlled analog and digital output signal to a signal generator (208) and a third control unit (212), and the signal generator (208) being configured for changing duty cycle ratio of amount of current flow in coils synchronizing the PWM signal and transferring to a second control unit (204); the second control unit (204) being configured for controlling the speed of the motor (124) according to the signal from 

 the signal generator (208) and transferring to the third control unit (212); and the third control unit (212) being configured for receiving the power from the battery and control signal from the first control unit (104) and the second control unit (204) and transferring the controlled battery power to the coil windings of the stator of the motor (124) for avoiding heat generation while generating high torque in a first mode and for regenerating high voltage and power at lower speed in a second mode. 2. The brushless low DC current high torque motor and regenerator (100) with controller as claimed in claim 1, wherein the motor 124 including an inner stator (304), the inner stator (304) being coaxially attached with the motor (124), an outer stator (308), the outer stator (308) being coaxially attached with the motor (124) and a rotor (312) being coaxially positioned such that rotor (308) being rotatable around the motor axis and positioned between inner and outer stators. 3. The brushless low DC current high torque motor and regenerator (100) with controller as claimed in claim 2, wherein, the inner stator (304) including a first plurality of teethes (316) and the outer stator (308) including a second plurality of teethes (320), both respectively having same even number of teethes and coil windings, being positioned such that, teethes having offset 

 by half (1/2) of angular pitch of the outer stator (308) and the inner stator (304) windings being in the manner where consecutive teethes are wind in opposite direction to create opposite polarity in consecutive teethes when power is applied for changing the current flow direction respective to rotor (312) position change. 4. The brushless low DC current high torque motor and regenerator (100) with controller as claimed in claim 2, wherein, the winding connections of the inner stator (304) and the outer stator (308) receiving controlled power from the third control unit (212), being selectively arranged in series or parallel, the series or parallel arrangement of winding being selected according to the requirement of size and power of the motor (124). The brushless low DC current high torque motor and regenerator (100) with controller as claimed in claim 1, wherein a sensor (332) being positioned on motor (124) for detecting the holes (328) on the rotor (312), for generating a respective pulse signal when the hole (328) being passed in front of the sensor (332), that pulse signal as the position signal being transferred to the first control unit (104). 5. The brushless low DC current high torque motor and regenerator (100) with controller as claimed in claim 2, wherein the plurality of magnets (324) is positioned on the rotor (312) and being arranged such that the direction of 

 opposite poles of the consecutive magnets being toward the center of the rotor (312). 6. The brushless low DC current high torque motor and regenerator (100) with controller as claimed in claim 1, wherein, the third control unit (212) for controlling the current by selecting the number of coils of the stators to be connected in series connection or selecting the number of coils to be connected in parallel connection to control current within the limit set by user and providing the controlled power to the motor (124) for controlling the speed of the motor (124) and for generating the torque. 7. The brushless low DC current high torque motor and regenerator (100) with controller as claimed in claim 2, wherein windings of both stators (304) and (308) being generated power while braking and transferring the generated power to third control unit (212) in the second mode of the regeneration. 8. The brushless low DC current high torque motor and regenerator (100) with controller as claimed in claim 1, wherein the first control unit (104) for finding speed and generating voltage in the second mode of the regeneration. 9. The brushless low DC current high torque motor and regenerator (100) with controller as claimed in claim 1, wherein the first control unit (104) being 

 for analysis and for determining the current flow direction through the inner stator (304) and the outer stator (308) of the motor (124). 10. The brushless low DC current high torque motor and regenerator (100) with controller as claimed in claim 1, the first controller (200) being for finding electrical angle of rotation of the rotor (312) with respective stators (304) and (308) from the signal received from the third input 116. 11. The brushless low DC current high torque motor and regenerator (100) with controller as claimed in claim 1, the first controller (200) for switching current flow direction in coils, for generating synchronous rotating magnetic field with respect to rotor magnet field and respective user inputs for rotating the motor in clock wise or counter clock wise direction for rotating the magnetic field. 12. The brushless low DC current high torque motor and regenerator (100) with controller as claimed in claim 1, wherein the third control unit (212) including a plurality of switches for connecting the coils of stators of the motor (124) in series and in parallel. 13. The brushless low DC current high torque motor and regenerator (100) with controller as claimed in claim 1, wherein in the second mode of regeneration the third control unit (212) being for receiving power from stator coils and for transferring controlled voltage power to the battery by 

 changing coils connection in different series and parallel combinations in the second mode of regeneration. 14. The brushless low DC current high torque motor and regenerator (100) with controller as claimed in claim 1, the signal generator (208) and first controller unit (200) receiving the first input (108) current being consumed and the second input (112) from user requirement of speed for analyzing and generating pulse width modulation (PWM) proportional to the user requirement and within current limits set by user.
